resultRaw.sql: SELECT d.building AS building, COUNT(d.id) AS department_count FROM public.departments AS d WHERE d.building IS NOT NULL GROUP BY d.building ORDER BY department_count DESC, building ASC;

issues: Visualization presents aggregated counts (department_count) per building rather than a mapping of each department to its building — this matches the plan but may differ from a literal reading of the question (which could request one row per department).

Vega-Lite x sort uses {"field":"department_count","op":"sum"} even though the query already returns aggregated counts; using sort without op (or sorting the input rows) would be clearer but is not a correctness issue.
Color is encoded by department_count with the legend suppressed; this color channel is redundant for a single quantitative measure on a bar chart and could be removed or adjusted for clarity.
metadata.sample shows 2 example rows while rowCount is 5 — the sample may be incomplete but does not affect correctness.
